question.label: Mentoring usefulness
question.name: You said mentoring is where you still feel useful — what is it giving you?
question.description: Drills into why mentoring stands out as the place he still feels useful, to surface what is missing elsewhere.

answer: I am not sure the work I am doing is the work that gets recognized.

assessment.note: He questions whether his current work is the kind that gets recognized, signaling mild discouragement and a muted sense of growth; also reveals a concrete recognition thread worth following.

new_queue.label: Recognition gap
new_queue.name: When you say 'gets recognized' — what kind of work do you mean?

new_queue.label: Feedback heard
new_queue.name: What feedback did you actually take in from that process?

new_queue.label: Support needed
new_queue.name: What would help you make the next few weeks feel more deliberate?

new_queue.label: Concrete next move
new_queue.name: Given what we've covered, where do you want to focus first?

history.answer_excerpt: I have been less present in rituals, yes.
Mentoring has been one place where I still feel useful.
I am not sure the work I am doing is the work that gets recognized.
I have been less present in rituals, yes.
After the promotion decision, I was not clear on what really needed to change.
After the promotion decision, I was not clear on what really needed to change.
I stayed focused on delivery because that felt controllable.
I am not sure the work I am doing is the work that gets recognized.
